description_long: This is a medium-sized bird with a streamlined body and predominantly dark brown to grayish plumage. The wings are long and pointed, lying flat against the body, and the tail is straight and tapers to a subtle point. The head is rounded with a short, hooked beak, and the eyes are closed, suggesting the bird is at rest or possibly a specimen. The underparts are lighter, with some mottled or patchy pale areas visible along the chest and belly. The legs are short and mostly hidden beneath the body, with dark coloration visible at the tips. The overall appearance is sleek and aerodynamic, typical of a predatory bird such as a falcon or hawk.
description: A streamlined, dark-feathered bird with pointed wings, a hooked beak, and a subtly pointed tail. Its plumage is mostly dark brown-gray with lighter mottling on the underside.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's streamlined body, pointed wings, and hooked beak, with subtle mottling on the underside.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the smooth, dark back and the symmetrical arrangement of the wings and tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the lighter underparts and the full length of the tail and beak.
description_view_3: Rear oblique view emphasizing the tail's shape and the folded wings, with some detail of the back feathers.
attribute_plumage_pattern: mottled with solid dark upperparts and lighter, patchy underparts
